channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Canadian PM Justin Trudeau plans to resign, official says
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau plans to announce his resignation Monday, an official said, in the face of rising discontent over his leadership, and after the abrupt departure of his finance minister signaled growing turmoil within his government.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Biden issues ban on offshore oil and gas drilling in most federal waters. Trump vows to undo it
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden is moving to ban new offshore oil and gas drilling in most U.S. coastal waters, a last-minute effort to block possible action by the incoming Trump administration to expand offshore drilling.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Wisconsin's Evers revives plan to enable voters to repeal and create state laws
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Wisconsin Gov. Tony Evers is reviving his plan to let voters repeal and create state laws without legislative involvement, this time through the state budget.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Harris will oversee certification of her defeat to Trump four years after he sparked Capitol attack
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Vice President Kamala Harris on Monday is set to preside over the certification of her defeat to Donald Trump four years after he tried to stop the very process that will now return him to the White House.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Congress is ready to certify Trump's election win, but his Jan. 6 legacy hangs over the day
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As Congress convenes during a winter storm to certify President-elect Donald Trump's election, the legacy of Jan. 6 hangs over the proceedings with an extraordinary fact: The candidate who tried to overturn the previous election won this time and is legitimately returning to power.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump’s lawyers ask judge to halt Friday's hush money case sentencing while they appeal to block it
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has asked a judge to halt this week’s sentencing in his New York hush money case while he appeals a ruling upholding the verdict.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Congress gears up for snowy certification of Trump's election win
item.description: Congress is gearing up for a snowy certification of President-elect Donald Trump’s election win on Monday, as Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) vows to plow ahead with the vote despite a snowstorm set to hit Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, KRON4

item.title: France's former President Sarkozy stands trial over alleged campaign funding by Libya's Gadhafi
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Former French President Nicolas Sarkozy 's trial started on Monday over alleged illegal financing of his 2007 presidential campaign by the government of then-Libyan leader Moammar Gadhafi.
item.dc:creator: NICOLAS VAUX-MONTAGNY and SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Congress gears up for snowy certification of Trump's election win
item.description: Congress is gearing up for a snowy certification of President-elect Trump’s election win on Monday, as Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) vows to plow ahead with the vote despite a snowstorm set to hit Washington, D.C. The House and Senate are scheduled to convene in a joint session at 1 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Divorce Day is Jan. 6. Here's why so many couples are breaking up
item.description: The first Monday of the year is known as Divorce Day, an unofficial term that points to the spike in divorce filings lawyers see in January.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Harris readies to certify Trump’s election win – and her decisive defeat
item.description: Kamala Harris is readying on Monday to certify the 2024 election results, affirming President-elect Trump’s victory as well as her decisive defeat to him after a whirlwind run for commander in chief.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Biden is traveling to New Orleans following the French Quarter attack that killed 14 and injured 30
item.description: President Joe Biden is heading to New Orleans as the city reels from the deadly New Year’s attack in which an Army veteran plowed a truck into revelers, killing 14 and injuring 30 more.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Long, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Lawsuit alleges ex-Fox Sports host Skip Bayless harassed hairstylist, offered her $1.5 million for sex
item.description: The complaint claims Fox executives fostered a hostile work environment that allowed senior managers and on-air personalities including Bayless to abuse workers without fear of punishment.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Texas pizzeria tips hat to highest bidder after petty theft uncovers another crime
item.description: A Texas pizzeria went viral on social media in efforts to replace stolen tip money, but ultimately uncovered another victim of the teenage bandits.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump’s Jan. 6 certification marks political comeback
item.description: President-elect Trump’s political comeback is set to be sealed Monday as Congress certifies his election victory, a full 180-degree turn from four years ago, when he sat stewing in the White House as his supporters violently stormed the Capitol.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Biden blocks some new offshore drilling ahead of Trump takeover
item.description: President Biden has blocked new offshore drilling for oil and gas in several parts of the country about two weeks before President-elect Trump takes office. Biden has announced that he’ll block new drilling off the entire East Coast, as well as California, Oregon, and Washington state.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida school employee arrested after he allegedly opened fire at Kentucky police officers
item.description: A Florida man was arrested after he allegedly fired a shot at a pair of officers in Kentucky while he was barricaded inside a bathroom in an Airbnb.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Californians advised to be on high alert for ‘Amber Alert’ scam
item.description: The California Highway Patrol says a new scam is circulating where people pose as Amber Alert "representatives" and attempt to gather personal information.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At New Orleans' St. Louis Cathedral, church leader asks for prayers for victims of truck attack
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — At a Mass celebrated Sunday at New Orleans' St. Louis Cathedral, just blocks from where a man sped down Bourbon Street in a deadly attack early on New Year's Day, a church leader acknowledged the sense of unease that remains and asked for prayers for victims and their families.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE and MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Bears rally for 24-22 win in Green Bay as Packers lose Love and Watson to injuries
item.description: Caleb Williams drove Chicago to Cairo Santos' 51-yard field goal as time expired and the Bears snapped a 10-game skid with a 24-22 victory over the playoff-bound Green Bay Packers, who lost quarterback Jordan Love and wide receiver Christian Watson to injuries on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: STEVE MEGARGEE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Detroit police officer killed in hit-and-run crash, person of interest at large
item.description: A Detroit police officer was killed on Sunday in a hit-and-run crash while driving home from work, and a person of interest remains at large.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Driving into Manhattan? That'll cost you, as new congestion toll starts Sunday
item.description: New York’s new toll for drivers entering the center of Manhattan debuted Sunday, meaning many people will pay $9 to access the busiest part of the Big Apple during peak hours.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: These winter driving 'tips' may be outdated and unnecessary, experts say
item.description: Driving in the winter is a different beast, especially when some of the most common advice for driving in the snow and cold isn't always right.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Rhodes, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Indie filmmaker Jeff Baena, Aubrey Plaza's husband, found dead at Los Angeles home
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Writer-director Jeff Baena, whose darkly comedic independent films included “The Little Hours” and who was married to his frequent creative collaborator Aubrey Plaza, has died. He was 47.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The FBI says the man responsible for New Year's truck attack visited New Orleans twice before
item.description: Shamsud-Din Jabbar, a U.S. citizen from Houston, also traveled to Cairo, Egypt, as well as Ontario, Canada, before the attack.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Jan. 6: Security preparations in DC on anniversary of Capitol Riots
item.description: Jan. 6, 2025, marks four years since the Capitol riots when former President Donald Trump denied the election results.
item.dc:creator: Hena Doba, NewsNation

item.title: British man and a nursing assistant were the last victims identified in New Orleans attack
item.description: The coroner's office said all the victims died from blunt force injuries. The suspect in the attack, Shamsud-Din Jabbar, was fatally shot in a firefight with police.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Browns fire offensive coordinator Ken Dorsey and O-line coach Andy Dickerson after 3-14 season
item.description: Hours after finishing 3-14 with a loss in the finale of a season that was expected to carry into the playoffs, Cleveland coach Kevin Stefanski fired offensive coordinator Ken Dorsey and offensive line coach Andy Dickerson.
item.dc:creator: TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Legacy of Jan. 6 prosecutions in flux with Trump presidency on horizon
item.description: The Justice Department’s prosecution of the Jan. 6, 2021, Capitol attack — hailed as one of the “largest, most complex, and most resource-intensive investigations” in its history — could soon go out with a fizzle.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Golden Globes: What to look for and how to watch on Sunday night
item.description: Hollywood is getting dressed up as the Golden Globes return for their annual champagne-soaked celebration of film and television that serves as the ceremonial start to awards season.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Opening a savings account? Here's what to know
item.description: Annual percentage yields (APYs) on top high-yield savings accounts are currently 4.5% and above, according to Bankrate.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, Fox 5

item.title: Why you shouldn't charge your phone to 100%, according to experts
item.description: It may be tempting to charge to 100%, but experts say 80% may be enough — and beneficial for your phone. Here's why.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New Orleans mourns victims of truck attack with tearful vigil and celebration of life
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — New Orleans mourned, wept and danced at a vigil Saturday evening along the famous thoroughfare where a man rammed a pickup truck into a crowd, killing and injuring revelers who were there to celebrate the new year.
item.dc:creator: JIM MUSTIAN, JACK BROOK, STEPHEN SMITH and SARA CLINE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tech law in 2025: a look ahead at AI, privacy and social media regulation under the new Trump administration
item.description: Artificial intelligence harms, problematic social media content, data privacy violations – the issues are the same, but the policymakers and regulators who deal with them are about to change.
item.dc:creator: Sylvia Lu, University of Michigan, Fox 5

item.title: What's on the table in 2025? Some predicted trends in food
item.description: As we tip into the New Year, we food writers are often tasked with putting on paper our predictions for what will be trending.
item.dc:creator: Katie Workman, WFLA, NBC

item.title: In-N-Out opening new restaurants across several states in 2025 and beyond: Here's where
item.description: The burger chain, which does not franchise its restaurants, currently keeps its operations (mostly) to the Western half of the country.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: Man hit person with frozen pizza rolls during fight in Walmart checkout line, police say
item.description: A man was arrested on New Year's Eve after he allegedly assaulted two people in a Walmart checkout line, hitting one of them with a bag of frozen pizza rolls, according to police in Conway, South Carolina.
item.dc:creator: Jordan White, WFLA, NBC
